observations.recommendation: 47. We recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to submit copies of perfected contracts along with the required relevant supporting documents within five (5) working days from execution/approval so that timely review by the team will performed and deficiencies will be corrected.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and it was agreed that the Local Chief Executive require:
The Municipal Accountant to comply with disclosure requirements for Biological Assets under IPSAS 27 and maintain Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks Ledger Card (WOABSLC) for all the livestock;
The Municipal Treasurer who is also the General Services Officer to maintain livestock cards for all livestock and to submit livestock inventory report to the audit team every July 31 and January 31 of each year; and
The Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer/Municipal Treasurer to reconcile both accounting and property records, respectively, before submission to COA for validation.

We recommended and it was agreed that Management require the Municipal Accountant-designate in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer to present the explanation of the changes between the original and final budget and differences between actual amounts and the budget amounts in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amount (SCBAA), for better understandability of the users of the FSs and other stakeholders.

We recommended and Management is amenable to re-evaluate its existing policy, for the determination of wage rate of Job Order/Contract of Service personnel and establish guidelines to adhere to the prescribed standards on labor practices.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Submit justification on the noted deviation of Items 4.3 and 4.4 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-01, on the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund of the LGU;
If not justified, replenish from the General Fund the expenditures inappropriately charged against the LDRRMF; and
Henceforth, strictly utilize the LDRRMF for projects and activities enumerated in Section 5 of the NDRRM-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommended and management assured to ensure that all radio communication equipment owned/used by the Municipality have pertinent permits and licenses in compliance with the NTC Radio Law so that its operations become legal and so with the incurrence of wages to the individual manning it.

We recommended and it was agreed that management require the recipients to refund the amount received and to exercise prudence in the disbursement of public funds in conformity with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-003 and Section 4(1) of PD No. 1445.

1. We recommended that Management submit justification on the noted deviation from Item 3 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2022 on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund of the annual IRA for the development projects of the LGU.

4. Cause the submission of the complete set of Financial Statements for CY 2023, pursuant to existing COA regulations; and

7. We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant – Designate the following remedial measures in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 to fairly present the Receivables as required under PPSAS No. 1:

• To issue demand letters to the persons concerned; and

• To file the appropriate request for authority to write-off for the dormant accounts duly supported with certified relevant documents validating the existence of the conditions, as applicable, such as the following:

a. Death certificate issued by the Philippine Statistics Office;

b. Certificate of no residence in the barangay of the municipality/city of last known address, Proof of exhaustion of all remedies to collect the receivable and demand to liquidate the cash advances and fund transfers, such as but not limited to copies of served or returned demand letters, and

c. Other justifications, like in the case of request for write-off due to loss of documents, the circumstances of the loss should be stated in the letter of request.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

9. Exercise the diligence of a good father of a family to ensure that tax payments be done accurately and promptly, withholding tax returns are supplied with correct information and required BIR forms must be filed and submitted to avoid incurrence of unnecessary tax penalties and to fully protect or conserve the government funds; and

10. Require the persons responsible or liable to refund the total amount of unnecessary tax penalties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

16. Set up a Repair and Maintenance Program for LGU’s Equipment and Motor Vehicles so that economical, efficient and effective repairs of government properties are undertaken.

17. Instruct the employee in-charge of processing the repair and maintenance of the LGU’s Equipment and Motor Vehicles to secure first the Pre-repair Inspection Report stating the extent of repair work to be done on the vehicle/equipment for repair enumerating the specific items of spare parts to be replaced to guide the Property Inspector/Inspection Committee in the Post-Repair Inspection. Post-Inspection Report must be prepared stating whether the work specified/required in the Pre-Inspection Report was undertaken or not.

18. Prepare and Maintain a Record of the LGU’s Equipment and Motor Vehicle repairs indicating therein the type of repair undertaken, the date when it was made or/and other pertinent data for the information and guidance of the COA, the LGU and others concerned.

19. Prepare Waste Materials Report on all replaced materials and spare parts.

20. Further, we recommend that management comply with the provisions of Section 9.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2023-004 on the attachment of complete documentary requirements relative to repairs and maintenance of government equipment and motor vehicles to facilitate validation of the legality, propriety, regularity and correctness of repair and maintenance transactions.
observations.reason:
Repair and Maintenance Program is not yet set up

Pre-repair and Post-repair Inspection Report are not prepared

Records of the LGU’s Equipment and Motor Vehicle repairs are not prepared and maintained

Attached documentary requirements were incomplete relative to the repairs and maintenance of the LGU’s equipment and motor vehicles

observations.recommendation:
We reiterate our previous audit recommendation that Management:

21. Expedite the filling in of the vacant positions for more effective administration of those offices and to be able to deliver the basic services needed by the LGU’s constituents efficiently and effectively.

22. Strictly observe Sections 443, 472 and 474 of Republic Act No. 7160 in appointing personnel, the Civil Service Rules in designating if necessary, and Section 27 of the Administrative Code of the Philippines in the hiring of applicants to the vacant positions.

23. Ensure that only qualified and competent personnel will be appointed or designated as head of office to ensure effective and efficient performance of office functions.

There are still vacant positions, such as:
1. Municipal Accountant
2. Municipal Health Officer
3. LDRRMO III
4. Registration Officer I
5. Agricultural Technologist
6. Social Welfare Assistant
7. Revenue Collection Clerk I

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to:

24. Comply with the provisions of RA 9184 in the procurement of its fuel, oil and lubricants with terms and conditions advantageous to the government as basis of entering a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA).

25. Require all officials and employees concerned and responsible in the preparation, approving of trip tickets as well as authorized passenger/s to ensure that driver’s trip ticket is duly and properly accomplished and approved before undertaking the trip and submission thereof, for reporting purposes, must consider the following:

a. Approved Driver’s Trip Tickets must be sequentially numbered for proper monitoring.

b. Required information/date in the Driver’s Trip Ticket should be filled-up accurately and duly approved by the Agency Head or authorized representative for proper monitoring of fuel consumption with complete information being filled out in every detail needed to support the claims for payment of fuel expenses. As to distance travelled, to determine the accurate consumption of fuel:
- All vehicles with functional odometers should record the kilometer reading before and after the trip.
- All vehicles with non-functional/defective odometer should indicate the approximate distance traveled including the no. of trips undertaken during the day. Immediate repair of the odometer should be considered.
- All travels with multiple trips from point of destination and back, the no. of trips should be indicated.
- Every vehicle should indicate the factor of its consumption for the distance travelled and/or the no. of liters for every hour of operation to determine the actual consumption of the vehicle.

3. All required reports should be prepared monthly and regularly.
- Require the signature/approval of all trips made and all passengers should sign the same.
- Monthly Report of official travels shall be prepared by the driver concerned summarizing in chronological order his trips for the month. For each vehicle, one report shall be prepared.
- Report of Fuel Consumption shall be prepared and shall show, among others the government motor vehicles utilized during the month, total distance travelled, total fuel used and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicle. The consumption report shall be submitted to the auditor to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the period.

26. All other measures should be exercised by all concerned to prevent wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.

observations.reason:

The lacking AIR and PAR/AIR or equivalent are not submitted to COA Office

34. Exercise the diligence of a good father of a family to ensure that tax payments be done accurately and promptly to avoid incurrence of unnecessary tax penalties and to fully protect or conserve the government funds.

1. Expedite the filling in of the vacant positions for more effective administration of those offices and to be able to deliver the basic services needed by the LGU’s constituents efficiently and effectively.

1. Comply with the provisions of RA 9184 in the procurement of its fuel, oil and lubricants with terms and conditions advantageous to the government as basis of entering a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA).

2. Require all officials and employees concerned and responsible in the preparation, approving of trip tickets as well as authorized passenger/s to ensure that driver’s trip ticket is duly and properly accomplished and approved before undertaking the trip and submission thereof, for reporting purposes, must consider the following:

a. Approved Driver’s Trip Tickets must be sequentially numbered for proper monitoring.

b. Required information/date in the Driver’s Trip Ticket should be filled-up accurately and duly approved by the Agency Head or authorized representative for proper monitoring of fuel consumption with complete information being filled out in every detail needed to support the claims for payment of fuel expenses. As to distance travelled, to determine the accurate consumption of fuel:
- All vehicles with functional odometers should record the kilometer reading before and after the trip.
- All vehicles with non-functional/defective odometer should indicate the approximate distance traveled including the no. of trips undertaken during the day. Immediate repair of the odometer should be considered.
- All travels with multiple trips from point of destination and back, the no. of trips should be indicated.
- Every vehicle should indicate the factor of its consumption for the distance travelled and/or the no. of liters for every hour of operation to determine the actual consumption of the vehicle.

c. All required reports should be prepared monthly and regularly.
- Require the signature/approval of all trips made and all passengers should sign the same.
- Monthly Report of official travels shall be prepared by the driver concerned summarizing in chronological order his trips for the month. For each vehicle, one report shall be prepared.
- Report of Fuel Consumption shall be prepared and shall show, among others the government motor vehicles utilized during the month, total distance travelled, total fuel used and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicle. The consumption report shall be submitted to the auditor to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the period.

3. All other measures should be exercised by all concerned to prevent wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the management to:

a. Require the SB Secretary to furnish the Accounting Office copies of the monthly SB resolution/ minutes and the later then include the said reports to support the monthly claims of the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) members;

b. Ensure the completeness of supporting documents, through the Municipal Accountant, on the monthly claims of salaries, PERA and RATA of the SB Members; and

c. Submit the lacking documents relating to the SB Personnel Benefits.

We recommend that the management, through the Municipal Accountant-Designate, to ensure the completeness of supporting documents on the monthly claims of salaries and wages of officers, employees, and JO personnel. In addition, we are requiring the management to submit the lacking documents relating to the payment of salaries and wages to officers, employees, and JO personnel.

We recommended Management to:

1. Require department heads to submit Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) for all the PAPs that will be included in the Annual Budget, not only limited to the Common-Use Supplies and Equipment;

2. Instruct the BAC Secretariat to review and consolidate all PPMPs into an indicative APP in the format approved by the GPPB, and revise the APP whenever necessary; and

3.  Furnish COA every year with a copy of the indicative APP (including the corresponding PPMPs) as approved by the HoPE, including any approved revisions.
observations.reason: Copy of approved APP and PPMP were not submitted to COA Office

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management evaluate its existing policy, if any, for the determination of wage rate of Job Order personnel and establish guidelines to adhere to the minimum standards on labor practices.

We recommended that Management:

1.  Ensure that the training needs of the GFPS are met to be able to effectively conduct gender analysis and use GAD tools in the planning and implementation of the GAD PPAs of the LGU;

2.  Direct the GFPS to refer to the Annexes D and E of the aforecited JMC 2013-01, as amended, in properly filling out the GPB and GDAR forms; and

3. Work on the proposed amendments to the local GAD Code to reflect provisions that directly address the needs of the LGU towards attainment of gender-responsive governance.
observations.reason: Amended local GAD Code were not submitted to COA Office for verification

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a.	Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to identify the unreconciled items amounting to ₱225,244.07 and adjust the books accordingly; and
Ensure monthly bank reconciliation is made to arrive at an adjusted CIB-LCCA balance.

We recommended that Management:
a. 	Discontinue granting cash advances to the Municipal Mayor other than travelling expenses and for the CIF to avoid any audit disallowances; and
b. 	Ensure that all CE and IE are fully accounted under the CIF only.

We recommended that Management follow through the titling of all its land properties to strengthen claim of ownership over subject properties and ensure that all future acquisitions of land properties be properly supported with Certificates of Title.

We recommended that Management:
Conduct reconciliation among PPE records for the recording of necessary adjustments identified as a result thereon; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Ledger Cards and update the Property and Stock Cards, respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation between their records.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit all the required reports on time to the Municipal Accountant, and the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team on the prescribed time for regular validation and verification;
Augment the workforce and personnel in the Accounting and Treasury Offices so that required reports are submitted promptly; and
Plan ahead the timing of physical count of all properties on date/s nearing yearend to give ample time for the reconciliation of records and avoid delay in the submission of the reports.

We recommended Management to:
Require department heads to submit Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) for all the PAPs that will be included in the Annual Budget, not only limited to the Common-Use Supplies and Equipment;
Instruct the BAC Secretariat to review and consolidate all PPMPs into an indicative APP in the format approved by the GPPB, and revise the APP whenever necessary; and
Furnish COA every year with a copy of the indicative APP (including the corresponding PPMPs) as approved by the HoPE, including any approved revisions.

We recommended that Management evaluate its existing policy, if any, for the determination of wage rate of Job Order personnel and establish guidelines to adhere to the minimum standards on labor practices.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure that the training needs of the GFPS are met to be able to effectively conduct gender analysis and use GAD tools in the planning and implementation of the GAD PPAs of the LGU;
Direct the GFPS to refer to the Annexes D and E of the aforecited JMC 2013-01, as amended, in properly filling out the GPB and GDAR forms; and
Work on the proposed amendments to the local GAD Code to reflect provisions that directly address the needs of the LGU towards attainment of gender-responsive governance.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management should:
1. Expedite the filling in of the vacant positions for more effective administration of those offices and to be able to deliver the basic services needed by the LGU’s constituents efficiently and effectively.
2. Strictly observe Sections 443, 472 and 474 of Republic Act No. 7160 in appointing personnel, the Civil Service Rules in designating if necessary, and Section 27 of the Administrative Code of the Philippines in the hiring of applicants to the vacant positions.
3. Ensure that only qualified and competent personnel will be appointed so that they can perform their duties and functions properly and timely.

We recommended that Management through the Accounting Office strictly implement the withholding of retention money or secure a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least one percent (1%) but not to exceed five percent (5%) of the total contract price on the procurement of goods to protect the municipality from any manufacturing defects and assure performance of warranty obligation, in compliance with Section 62.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 as amended by GPPB Resolution No. 30-2017.

We recommended that the Municipality should monitor the target dates of completion of all projects. Immediate submission of approved requests for extension of contract time is also enjoined. And when necessary, they should initiate the computation and subsequent collection of liquidated damages.

We recommended that Management:

1.	Stop the grant of additional cash advances to erring officials and employees until their previous cash advances are fully liquidated and properly accounted for in the books and until appropriate sanctions are imposed for the unjustified failure of the concerned AOs to liquidate their cash advances within the reglementary period.
2.	Direct the Accountable Officers to liquidate their long outstanding cash advances and exert all efforts to enforce their settlement. Upon exhausting all efforts necessary for the settlement of these advances, request for the write-off of these accounts along with the necessary supporting documents in compliance with Section 8.3.b of COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

3.	Ensure that succeeding cash advances are in conformance to the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 to avoid sanctions and to attain a fair presentation of the relevant accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and that Management:

Stop the practice of incorporating expenditure programs in the LDRRMF Plan which are not directly related to the implementation of DRRM Programs. Budgetary requirements for the Personnel Services, Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses and Capital Outlay of the Office should be sourced from the General Fund of the LGU pursuant to Section 4.3 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014; and

Strictly observe the guidelines in the allocation and utilization of the LDRRMF pursuant to NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to submit copies of perfected contracts along with the required relevant supporting documents within five (5) working days from execution/approval so that timely review by the team will performed and deficiencies will be corrected;

1.	Provide space/room for the BAC and BAC Secretariat for their meetings and recordkeeping to protect the confidentiality of their records; and
2.	Accomplish the Purchase Orders with due diligence in order avoid confusion and difficulty in demanding compliance and enforcing penalties.

We recommended that Management requires the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to adhere to the required number of observers in the procurement activities and ensure that only qualified observers are invited pursuant to Section 13.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

We recommended that the municipality should (1) prepare an LDRRMFIP for the DRRM Program following the prescribed format and furnish the DILG - Local Government Operations Office; (2) incorporate the prior years’ unspent LDRRM Funds; (3) fully implement all the programs, activities and projects (P/A/Ps) included in the LDRRMFIP; and (4) Prepare a monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

We recommended that Management:
Submit a copy of any Purchase Order along with the required supporting documents within five (5) working days from issuance thereof in compliance with COA Circular 2009-001; and 
Ensure that Notices of Delivery are forwarded to the Auditor within 24 hours from Acceptance of the Purchases.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant compute the depreciation expense to properly reflect the income for the period. Moreover, depreciation schedule should be made to monitor the same. All previous depreciation expenses not recognized should also be computed and adjusting entry should be made with a debit to Prior Period Adjustment and credit to Accumulated Depreciation.
We recommended that Management should exhaust all possible means to secure the TCTs/OCTs of its properties as soon as possible in accordance to Presidential Decree 1445.

We recommended that management direct the Accountant to submit the monthly reports in accordance with the prescribed period in order to facilitate review and any deficiency will be communicated timely and accordingly.

We had recommended to adhere strictly on the rules and regulations provided in the revised IRR of Republic Act 9184 to avoid sanctions of non-compliance. In addition, the BAC secretariat must be prudent in her duties as enumerated in the said act.

We had recommended the management to strictly observe the reglementary period of submission so that timely review and verification could be obtained and relevant decision making will be achieved.

We had also recommended to hire additional accounting staff if necessary to backtrack the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements and exhaust all possible ways and means to retrieve the data from previous years.

We recommended that Management:

Stop the grant of additional cash advances to erring officials and employees until their previous cash advances are fully liquidated and properly accounted for in the books and until appropriate sanctions are imposed for the unjustified failure of the concerned AOs to liquidate their cash advances within the reglementary period.
Direct the Accountable Officers to liquidate their long outstanding cash advances and exert all efforts to enforce their settlement. Upon exhausting all efforts necessary for the settlement of these advances, request for the write-off of these accounts along with the necessary supporting documents in compliance with Section 8.3.b of COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Ensure that succeeding cash advances are in conformance to the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 to avoid sanctions and to attain a fair presentation of the relevant accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management requires the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to adhere to the required number of observers in the procurement activities and ensure that only qualified observers are invited pursuant to Section 13.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.
We recommended that Management should exhaust all possible means to secure the TCTs/OCTs of its properties as soon as possible in accordance to Presidential Decree 1445.

We recommended that management direct the Accountant to submit the monthly reports in accordance with the prescribed period in order to facilitate review and any deficiency will be communicated timely and accordingly.

We had recommended the management to strictly observe the reglementary period of submission so that timely review and verification could be obtained and relevant decision making will be achieved.

We had also recommended to hire additional accounting staff if necessary to backtrack the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements and exhaust all possible ways and means to retrieve the data from previous years.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Require the Inventory Committee to expedite the completion of the physical count of all supplies and properties, and prepare and submit the corrected reports as required;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain the property and stock cards of the properties of the LGU, and conduct reconciliation of such records and the various Reports on Physical Count as against Accounting records, to determine the completeness, existence and accuracy of the subject accounts.

Recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management should:
Expedite the filling in of the vacant positions for more effective administration of those offices and to be able to deliver the basic services needed by the LGU’s constituents efficiently and effectively.
Strictly observe Sections 443, 472 and 474 of Republic Act No. 7160 in appointing personnel, the Civil Service Rules in designating if necessary, and Section 27 of the Administrative Code of the Philippines in the hiring of applicants to the vacant positions.
Ensure that only qualified and competent personnel will be appointed so that they can perform their duties and functions properly and timely.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management through the Accounting Office strictly implement the withholding of retention money or secure a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least one percent (1%) but not to exceed five percent (5%) of the total contract price on the procurement of goods to protect the municipality from any manufacturing defects and assure performance of warranty obligation, in compliance with Section 62.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 as amended by GPPB Resolution No. 30-2017.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality should monitor the target dates of completion of all projects. Immediate submission of approved requests for extension of contract time is also enjoined. And when necessary, they should initiate the computation and subsequent collection of liquidated damages.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Prioritize and allocate funds for the capability building programs on GAD of all members of the GFPS and seek assistance of a gender analysis specialist/expert in the use of HGDG;
Work on the creation and staffing of the Municipal GAD Office, required under the GAD Code.

We recommended and that Management:

Stop the practice of incorporating expenditure programs in the LDRRMF Plan which are not directly related to the implementation of DRRM Programs. Budgetary requirements for the Personnel Services, Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses and Capital Outlay of the Office should be sourced from the General Fund of the LGU pursuant to Section 4.3 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014; and

Strictly observe the guidelines in the allocation and utilization of the LDRRMF pursuant to NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended that Management:

Expedite the filling in of the vacant positions for more effective administration of those offices and to be able to deliver the basic services needed by the LGU’s constituents efficiently and effectively.

Strictly observe Sections 443, 472 and 474 of Republic Act No. 7160 in appointing personnel, the Civil Service Rules in designating if necessary, and Section 27 of the Administrative Code of the Philippines in the hiring of applicants to the vacant positions.

Ensure that only qualified and competent personnel will be appointed so that they can perform their duties and functions properly and timely.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to submit copies of perfected contracts along with the required relevant supporting documents within five (5) working days from execution/approval so that timely review by the team will performed and deficiencies will be corrected;

Provide space/room for the BAC and BAC Secretariat for their meetings and recordkeeping to protect the confidentiality of their records; and
Accomplish the Purchase Orders with due diligence in order avoid confusion and difficulty in demanding compliance and enforcing penalties.

We recommended that the municipality should (1) prepare an LDRRMFIP for the DRRM Program following the prescribed format and furnish the DILG - Local Government Operations Office; (2) incorporate the prior years’ unspent LDRRM Funds; (3) fully implement all the programs, activities and projects (P/A/Ps) included in the LDRRMFIP; and (4) Prepare a monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

We recommended that Management:
Submit a copy of any Purchase Order along with the required supporting documents within five (5) working days from issuance thereof in compliance with COA Circular 2009-001; and 

Ensure that Notices of Delivery are forwarded to the Auditor within 24 hours from Acceptance of the Purchases.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC to revise and submit the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) in the prescribed format to the GPPB as mandated under Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 as amended by GPPB Resolution No. 08-2017.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant compute the depreciation expense to properly reflect the income for the period. Moreover, depreciation schedule should be made to monitor the same. All previous depreciation expenses not recognized should also be computed and adjusting entry should be made with a debit to Prior Period Adjustment and credit to Accumulated Depreciation.

We recommend the Municipality should submit a revised timeline for approval by the Department of Interior and Local Government of Region X.  In addition, we recommend them to install a community billboard in the premises of the Municipal Hall.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Agriculture Officer  and the Municipal Accountant to retrieve the old files of the property records and consequently conduct physical inventory and make reconciliation on all the Agency’s Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) as well as the Livestock and Crops Inventory to establish correct and reliable amount in the books of accounts of the Municipality so that the reflected amount in the financial statement will also be reliable.  Issue directive to the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Agriculturist that they should coordinate each other in the monitoring and control of the Livestock Inventory and Work/Other Animal in order to maintain proper and correct amount of the Livestock and Work/Other Animal Accounts.

We had recommended to adhere strictly on the rules and regulations provided in the revised IRR of Republic Act 9184 to avoid sanctions of non-compliance. In addition, the BAC secretariat must be prudent in her duties as enumerated in the said act.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC to revise and submit the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) in the prescribed format to the GPPB as mandated under Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 as amended by GPPB Resolution No. 08-2017.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant compute the depreciation expense to properly reflect the income for the period. Moreover, depreciation schedule should be made to monitor the same. All previous depreciation expenses not recognized should also be computed and adjusting entry should be made with a debit to Prior Period Adjustment and credit to Accumulated Depreciation.
observations.reason: Depreciation still not recognized. Accountant said they cannot recognize yet due to loss of records on the acquisition dates and will endeavor to comply in 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should exhaust all possible means to secure the TCTs/OCTs of its properties as soon as possible in accordance to Presidential Decree 1445.
observations.reason: Management commented that actions has already been taken to secure OCT/TCT but they are challenged to provide supporting documents for their claims. However, the management has assured the team that they would take all the necessary steps to register the subject lots during the year. They will be submitting update for CY 2018.

Management explained that they're having difficulty preparing POW for their projects since they lack capable and able personnel. They only outsource this task. They will just seek assistance from DPWH.

We recommended that Management requires the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to adhere to the required number of observers in the procurement activities and ensure that only qualified observers are invited pursuant to Section 13.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

We recommended that the municipality should (1) prepare an LDRRMFIP for the DRRM Program following the prescribed format and furnish the DILG - Local Government Operations Office; (2) incorporate the prior years’ unspent LDRRM Funds; (3) fully implement all the programs, activities and projects (P/A/Ps) included in the LDRRMFIP; and (4) Prepare a monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

We recommended that Management: Submit a copy of any Purchase Order along with the required supporting documents within five (5) working days from issuance thereof in compliance with COA Circular 2009-001; and 
Ensure that Notices of Delivery are forwarded to the Auditor within 24 hours from Acceptance of the Purchases.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned personnel to discontinue the practice of increasing the employer’s share of Pag-IBIG contribution that is more than what is required by the aforementioned law, to avoid said unnecessary expenditure that may deplete the local funds which may result to audit disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Vice - Mayor, in cooperation of the Human Resource and Management Officer and the Sangguniang Bayan, to fill in the vacant position as soon as possible in conformity with Article 117, 121 and 122 of the IRR of RA 7160.
observations.reason: The Office of the Municipal Vice-Mayor has created a resolution to open the position so that it will be filled in 2018. COA-ATL requested letter update and copy of said resolution.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipality should submit a revised timeline for approval by the Department of Interior and Local Government of Region X.  In addition, we recommend them to install a community billboard in the premises of the Municipal Hall.
observations.reason: The project has already been implemented and percentage of completion is at 75% accordinf to Management. No. of days of extension is 254 CD. Estimated completion date is by  final week of March 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop granting cash advances without or with incomplete supporting documents and adhere strictly to the rules and regulations on granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to avoid sanctions of noncompliance.

We recommended the immediate liquidation and/refund of the said cash advances and the suspension of succeeding grants of cash advances to Accountable Officers with outstanding unliquidated cash advances parallel to the aforementioned guidelines and laws.

Considering that an AOM related to the proper and timely liquidation had already been issued, we urged that the Accountant should withhold the salary of accountable officers, who refuse to liquidate the cash advances immediately, especially those cash advances which have been outstanding for more than One (1) month from its prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular 90-331 until liquidation is effected. Further, we recommend not to allow piecemeal liquidation of cash advances.
observations.reason: According to Management, 2 demand letters were sent to concerned personnel in 2018. Final demand letter has not been sent yet. Also, Accountant stated that less than 1 million are left outstanding.

observations.recommendation: Wealso recommended that Management to instruct the Supply Officer and designated Driver to prepare diligently the required Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels, respectively, as required under the existing rules and regulation. Control and monitoring of fuel consumption should be made in order to ascertain the validity and propriety on the expenses incurred on fuel, oil and gasoline and to minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures of fuel consumption.
observations.reason: Still for submission. Unsigned yet by Mayor, according to personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should exhaust all possible means to secure the TCTs/OCTs of its properties as soon as possible in accordance to Presidential Decree 1445.
observations.reason: Update letter yet to be submitted. COA-ATL reminded them to provide update on actions taken.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to compute the depreciation expense to properly reflect the income for the period. Moreover, depreciation schedule should be made to monitor the same. All previous depreciation expenses not recognized should also be computed and adjusting entry should be made with a debit to Prior Year adjustment and credit to Accumulated Depreciation.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Agriculture Officer  and the Municipal Accountant to retrieve the old files of the property records and consequently conduct physical inventory and make reconciliation on all the Agency’s Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) as well as the Livestock and Crops Inventory to establish correct and reliable amount in the books of accounts of the Municipality so that the reflected amount in the financial statement will also be reliable.  Issue directive to the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Agriculturist that they should coordinate each other in the monitoring and control of the Livestock Inventory and Work/Other Animal in order to maintain proper and correct amount of the Livestock and Work/Other Animal Accounts.

We had recommended the management to strictly observe the reglementary period of submission so that timely review and verification could be obtained and relevant decision making will be achieved.

We had also recommendedto hire additional accounting staff if necessary to backtrack the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements and exhaust all possible ways and means to retrieve the data from previous years.

Stop the practice of charging to 20% Development Fund programs, projects and activities that are not in accordance with the policies and guidelines set on the utilization of such fund in order to maximize the benefits that can be derived from the fund.

	The Local Chief Executive shall ensure the strict utilization of the fund in accordance with the issued guidelines to avoid subsequent disallowances in audit and the sanctions laid down under Item 5 of the Joint Memorandum.

We recommended that Management shall stop granting excessive daily wage rate to casual employees. Strict adherence to existing law, rules and regulations shall be followed. Require the concern casual employees to refund the excess claimed by them.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant compute the depreciation expense to properly reflect the income for the period. Moreover, depreciation schedule should be made to monitor the same. All previous depreciation expenses not recognized should also be computed and adjusting entry should be made with a debit to Prior Period Adjustment and credit to Accumulated Depreciation.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct all officials and employees to submit all liquidation reports with the corresponding transmittals directly to the Municipal Accountant; wherein the latter shall sign or stamp “Received”all the transmittals and liquidation reports indorsed.
Further, he should direct the other heads of office and/or employees to refuse any liquidation reports indorsed to them in order to mitigate the risk of loss.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Local Finance Committee to strictly comply with the PS limitation prescribed under Section 325 of R.A 7160 in the preparation of annual budget. Moreover, the concerned officials must refrain from providing additional PS expenditures, until such time that the PS cap has been observed. Finally, require the concerned municipal officials and employees to refund all the additional PS expenses granted to them in excess of the PS limitation.
We recommended that Management should be more mindful of their priorities and should avoid incurring IUEEU expenditures in order to avoid sanctions.

We recommended to instruct the BAC to prepare the procurement monitoring report and submit to the GPPB in accordance with the time frame stipulated by the law in order to effectively serve its purpose.

We had recommended to adhere strictly on the rules and regulations provided in the revised IRR of Republic Act 9184 to avoid sanctions of non-compliance. In addition, the BAC secretariat must be prudent in her duties as enumerated in the said act.

We recommended to strictly adhere to the rules and regulations in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to avoid sanctions of noncompliance. Further, we recommend that the accountant shall withhold the salary of the accountable officers/employees concerned until full liquidation of the same. 

We also recommended to liquidate immediately the cash advances granted before December 31, 2011.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to compute the depreciation expense to properly reflect the income for the period. Moreover, depreciation schedule should be made to monitor the same. All previous depreciation expenses not recognized should also be computed and adjusting entry should be made with a debit to Prior Year adjustment and credit to Accumulated Depreciation.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Agriculture Officer  and the Municipal Accountant to retrieve the old files of the property records and consequently conduct physical inventory and make reconciliation on all the Agency’s Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) as well as the Livestock and Crops Inventory to establish correct and reliable amount in the books of accounts of the Municipality so that the reflected amount in the financial statement will also be reliable.  Issue directive to the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Agriculturist that they should coordinate each other in the monitoring and control of the Livestock Inventory and Work/Other Animal in order to maintain proper and correct amount of the Livestock and Work/Other Animal Accounts.

We recommended to instruct the BAC to prepare the procurement monitoring report and submit to the GPPB in accordance with the time frame stipulated by the law in order to effectively serve its purpose.

We  had recommended the management to strictly observe the reglementary period of submission so that timely review and verification could be obtained and relevant decision making will be achieved.

We had also  recommended to hire additional accounting staff if necessary to backtrack the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements and exhaust all possible ways and means to retrieve the data from previous years.

Stop the practice of charging to 20% Development Fund programs, projects and activities that are not in accordance with the policies and guidelines set on the utilization of such fund in order to maximize the benefits that can be derived from the fund.

	The Local Chief Executive shall ensure the strict utilization of the fund in accordance with the issued guidelines to avoid subsequent disallowances in audit and the sanctions laid down under Item 5 of the Joint Memorandum.

We recommended that Management shall stop granting excessive daily wage rate to casual employees. Strict adherence to existing law, rules and regulations shall be followed. Require the concern casual employees to refund the excess claimed by them.

We recommended that management stop granting cash advances without or with incomplete supporting documents and adhere strictly to the rules and regulations on granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to avoid sanctions of noncompliance.

We recommended the immediate liquidation and/refund of the said cash advances and the suspension of succeeding grants of cash advances to Accountable Officers with outstanding unliquidated cash advances parallel to the aforementioned guidelines and laws.
Considering that an AOM related to the proper and timely liquidation had already been issued, we urged that the Accountant should withhold the salary of accountable officers, who refuse to liquidate the cash advances immediately, especially those cash advances which have been outstanding for more than One (1) month from its prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular No. 90-331 until liquidation is effected. Further, we recommend not to allow piecemeal liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended reclassifying the above expenditures to its proper responsibility center and depositing the corresponding amount back to the 20% Development Fund account for its proper utilization under the revised approved budget for CY 2016. Further, prevent from incurring the same in the future to avoid disallowance in audit.
We recommended the management to submit the disbursement vouchers within the period prescribed by these COA Circulars in order to avoid sanctions.

We recommended that management direct the Accountant to submit the monthly reports in accordance with the prescribed period in order to facilitate review and any deficiency will be communicated timely and accordingly.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management submits to the Auditor, for review and evaluation, copies of approved contract/purchase/letter orders and all the supporting documents forming part thereof within the prescribed period in order to facilitate timely inspection of deliveries and any defects/deficiencies manifested therein could be immediately corrected.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Local Finance Committee to strictly comply with the PS limitation prescribed under Section 325 of R.A 7160 in the preparation of annual budget. Moreover, the concerned officials must refrain from providing additional PS expenditures, until such time that the PS cap has been observed. Finally, require the concerned municipal officials and employees to refund all the additional PS expenses granted to them in excess of the PS limitation.
We recommended that Management should be more mindful of their priorities and should avoid incurring IUEEU expenditures in order to avoid sanctions.

We recommended the Municipality to mark all of their vehicles with “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY,” together with the corresponding name of the agency operating/using the motor vehicles, in accordance with the instructions of COA Ciruclar 77-61, in order to avoid disallowances as instructed in COA Circular 75-6.
We also recommended that Management to instruct the Supply Officer and designated Driver to prepare diligently the required Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels, respectively, as required under the existing rules and regulation. Control and monitoring of fuel consumption should be made in order to ascertain the validity and propriety on the expenses incurred on fuel, oil and gasoline and to minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures of fuel consumption.
We recommended that Management should exhaust all possible means to secure the TCTs/OCTs of its properties as soon as possible in accordance to Presidential Decree 1445.

We had recommended to adhere strictly on the rules and regulations provided in the revised IRR of Republic Act 9184 to avoid sanctions of non-compliance. In addition, the BAC secretariat must be prudent in her duties as enumerated in the said act.
